behavior_summary: Provides the in-game behavior for AIRFOCUS items: when a player 'looks' at (examines) the item it emits a short identifying text ('bark') naming the specific focus represented by the item (examples: 'sextant focus', 'pointing hand focus', etc.).
When the item is 'used' it maps the item's frame/index to a corresponding AIRSPEL procedure and spawns that spell/process (passing the caller pid and occasional small integer arguments).

Both handlers perform initial process setup/housekeeping (inferred set info / process exclude) before acting, and they free temporary strings after barking or synchronize with spawned processes before returning/suspending.
notable_control_flow: U8P_AIRFOCUS::AIRFOCUS::look: perform process setup, call Item::getFrame repeatedly and compare against a sequence of constants (0x00..0x11); for the matched frame allocate/push a literal string and call Item::bark(item, string); free the string and perform an implies/suspend sequence before returning.

U8P_AIRFOCUS::AIRFOCUS::use: perform process setup, initialize a small local byte ([BP-02h]) to 0, call Item::getFrame and for each recognized frame spawn a specific AIRSPEL process (different entry addresses per frame). Some spawn calls include extra small integer args. After spawn the code synchronizes/implies with the spawned process and then suspends/returns.
If no frame matches, both handlers fall through to a quiet return (no bark or spawn).
open_questions: Exact runtime semantics and side-effects of the 'set info / process exclude' sequence invoked at start of both handlers (appears to be process metadata/housekeeping but exact keys and effects are not explicit).
Precise mapping from each frame value to the full gameplay effect of the spawned AIRSPEL (we see spawn addresses but not the spell implementation or its gameplay description).
Whether the [BP-02h] local initialized in AIRFOCUS::use is ever mutated/used by other code-paths not present in this excerpt (its purpose is inferred as a small flag/argument).

main_function.name: U8P_AIRFOCUS::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to the 'U8P_AIRFOCUS::look' (examine) event for an AIRFOCUS item by reporting which specific focus the item represents via a short text bark; perform initial process setup/housekeeping beforehand.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (examine/U8P_AIRFOCUS::look) - invoked when the player or another actor inspects the AIRFOCUS item.
Requires the item instance (inferred at BP+06h) and the caller pid to target/broadcast the bark.

function_map.name: U8P_AIRFOCUS::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_AIRFOCUS::look handler that emits a short identifying string for the focus item based on its frame.

function_map.name: U8P_AIRFOCUS::use
function_map.role: Use handler that spawns the AIRSPEL process corresponding to the focus's effect (frame→spell mapping), performing spawn args and synchronization.

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Query the item's frame/index to determine which focus variant this instance represents.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit a short text/message from the item to a process/player (used to show the focus name when looked at).

function_map.name: spawn (AIRSPEL::<addr>)
function_map.role: Spawn/launch the spell/process implementing the focus's active effect; many addresses are used depending on frame value.

function_map.name: FREE::free (string cleanup)
function_map.role: Free temporary string buffers allocated for bark messages after they are passed to Item::bark.

function_map.name: U8P_AIRFOCUS::engine:set info / process exclude
function_map.role: Process-level setup/housekeeping invoked at the start of handlers (likely configures process metadata or exclusion from normal processing).

external_calls.purpose: Read the item's frame value to decide which focus name to bark.
Cause the item to emit a short identifying string (e.g., 'sextant focus') to the inspecting process/player.
Free the temporary string buffer allocated for the bark message after calling Item::bark.
Determine which spell (AIRSPEL) to spawn based on the item's frame.
Spawn the spell/process that implements the focus's active ability; passes caller pid and occasional small integer args.
Initial process setup/housekeeping performed before spawn decisions (exact semantics inferred).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the in-game behavior for AIRFOCUS items: when a player 'looks' at (examines) the item it emits a short identifying text ('bark') naming the specific focus represented by the item (examples: 'sextant focus', 'pointing hand focus', etc.).
